question: What strong local teacher models are feasible on two NVIDIA B200 GPUs for mathematically standard OPD, and what teacher models are used in current OPD examples?

local_hardware_observation.note: GPUs 4 and 5 were still occupied by the v13 LlamaFactory training run when checked; GPUs 6 and 7 were essentially idle.

current_opd_teacher_examples.teacher_backend: SGLang external server or Megatron-loaded teacher
current_opd_teacher_examples.details: Student trains on its own rollouts; teacher token log-probs are used as an OPD KL penalty on top of GRPO/PPO/REINFORCE-style advantage estimators.

current_opd_teacher_examples.teacher: Qwen3-32B for heterogeneous SGLang mode; same-architecture teacher for Megatron mode
current_opd_teacher_examples.details: SGLang teacher supports 32B -> 8B heterogeneous distillation; Megatron teacher currently requires teacher/student architecture shape compatibility.

current_opd_teacher_examples.source: Thinking Machines Tinker cookbook basic OPD recipe
current_opd_teacher_examples.teacher: Qwen/Qwen3-8B by default, often from a stronger post-trained or checkpointed policy
current_opd_teacher_examples.details: Uses reverse KL on student-sampled trajectories. Their blog also discusses Qwen3-32B as the reasoning teacher and self/previous-policy teachers for behavior recovery.

current_opd_teacher_examples.teacher: Qwen/Qwen3-32B for DeepMath, Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 for Tulu3
current_opd_teacher_examples.details: Shows dataset-specific teachers and confirms that very large MoE teachers are used when logprob serving is available.

current_opd_teacher_examples.source: Thinking Machines Harbor multi-turn tool-use recipe
current_opd_teacher_examples.details: Multi-turn tool-use OPD masks environment/tool tokens and trains only student-generated tokens against teacher KL.
Report states smaller models use strong-to-weak distillation with both off-policy and on-policy transfer from larger models; exact public scripts are not provided in the report.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: About 64GB weights before KV cache. Runs comfortably on one B200 or two B200 with large context and high batch. This is the most common public OPD teacher in slime/Relax examples.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Primary safe teacher for first true OPD implementation.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: Same 32B memory class, strong reasoning style, Qwen-family tokenizer/model compatibility is favorable.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Good reasoning teacher ablation, especially for 2Wiki/SimpleQA reasoning traces.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: 30B total / 3B activated MoE. Strong recent Qwen3 agent/tool-use and long-context model, very cheap active compute relative to dense 32B.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Best practical teacher candidate for agent/tool behavior if vLLM/SGLang logprob support is stable.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: About 140-144GB weights before KV cache, within 2xB200. Long context and high batching reduce available KV cache, but feasible with tensor parallel 2.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Useful if 32B teachers are too weak, but less current than Qwen3-30B/32B.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: 70B dense fits in 2xB200 and is a strong reasoning teacher, but tokenizer/style mismatch with Qwen student may complicate token-level KL rendering.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Use as judge/generator or secondary teacher, not first OPD teacher.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: BF16 weights are about 470GB and do not fit on two B200s. FP8 weights are about 235GB before KV cache and may fit with careful engine settings; INT4 is much easier but logprob quality and framework support must be validated.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Best-quality target teacher if we can serve FP8/quantized logprobs reliably; not the fastest first implementation.

two_b200_feasible_teachers.precision: INT4 maybe fits weights only, FP8/BF16 no
two_b200_feasible_teachers.why: Even INT4 is about 335GB before KV cache/overhead, leaving too little headroom on two B200s for long context and batching.
two_b200_feasible_teachers.recommended_for_sii: Do not use for local OPD on two B200s.

recommendation.first_teacher: Qwen/Qwen3-32B or Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 served by SGLang/vLLM on GPUs 6 and 7
recommendation.reason: These fit comfortably, match public OPD precedent, have Qwen-style tool/chat rendering close to the 9B student, and can provide forced logprobs for student trajectories.
recommendation.stretch_teacher: Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 in FP8/INT4 if serving logprobs is stable
recommendation.avoid_initially: Closed Azure GPT-5.4 as token-level teacher, because this endpoint returns logprobs only for its own generated tokens and did not support completions echo/forced scoring.

implementation_notes_for_sii: Use teacher logprobs only on student-generated assistant/action/final-answer tokens; mask user/system/tool observation tokens.
Keep teacher/student chat template rendering aligned. Same-family Qwen teachers reduce tokenizer/template mismatch risk.
For agent OPD, Qwen3 Thinking teachers may have tool-use caveats in vLLM/SGLang if reasoning_content is stripped; pass full content or prefer Instruct/non-thinking mode for tool-call scoring.
A multi-teacher setup is attractive: Qwen3-32B or DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B for 2Wiki/SimpleQA reasoning, Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct or Qwen3-235B-A22B for agent/tool behavior if available.
sources_checked: Qwen3 README and Qwen3 Technical Report HTML
Hugging Face public model metadata for Qwen3, QwQ, and DeepSeek distill models
